req.title: Rate limiting
req.statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after.

doc.reasoning: Rate limiting is a critical security and fairness control in financial APIs. The requirement specifies a hard limit of 60 requests/minute per user with explicit 429 status and retry-after header on overage. Test suite applies EP (partition by request count ranges), BVA (test at 59, 60, 61 requests), NEG (rejection scenarios), and STC (state transitions from allowed to throttled). All tests are deterministic, self-contained, and idempotent through use of unique user tokens and isolated request sequences.

tests.name: test_rate_limit_under_threshold_succeeds
preconditions: Rate limit counter is reset for user
actions: Send 30 requests within 60 seconds to any endpoint
Use same user token for all requests
Assert each response status
trace: R-FIN-024: verify requests below 60/min threshold are allowed

tests.name: test_rate_limit_at_boundary_60_succeeds
preconditions: Rate limit window is exactly 60 seconds
Rate limit counter is reset for user
actions: Send exactly 60 requests within 60 seconds
Use same user token for all requests
Measure response times and status codes
trace: R-FIN-024: verify boundary case at exactly 60 requests/minute
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest
import time

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('reset_rate_limit')
def test_rate_limit_at_boundary_60_succeeds(api_base_url, auth_token):
    endpoint = f"{api_base_url}/accounts"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    start_time = time.time()
    
    for i in range(60):
        resp = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
        assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
        assert "retry-after" not in resp.headers
        time.sleep(0.95)  # ~57 sec total for 60 requests
    
    elapsed = time.time() - start_time
    assert elapsed < 65, f"All 60 requests completed in {elapsed}s"

tests.name: test_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429_with_retry_after
preconditions: User has already consumed 60 requests in current minute
Rate limit counter is reset for user
actions: Send 61 requests within 60 seconds
Use same user token for all requests
Inspect response status and headers on 61st request
trace: R-FIN-024: verify overage rejection with 429 and retry-after
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest
import time

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('reset_rate_limit')
def test_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429_with_retry_after(api_base_url, auth_token):
    endpoint = f"{api_base_url}/accounts"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    
    for i in range(60):
        resp = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
        assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
        time.sleep(0.9)
    
    resp_61 = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
    assert resp_61.status_code == 429
    assert "retry-after" in resp_61.headers
    retry_after = int(resp_61.headers["retry-after"])
    assert retry_after > 0, f"retry-after={retry_after} must be positive"

tests.name: test_rate_limit_burst_rejection_immediate
preconditions: Rate limit counter is reset for user
actions: Send 80 requests as fast as possible (minimal delay)
Use same user token for all requests
Record status code of each request
trace: R-FIN-024: verify burst attack is rejected at threshold
Negative path: rapid sequential requests exceed limit
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('reset_rate_limit')
def test_rate_limit_burst_rejection_immediate(api_base_url, auth_token):
    endpoint = f"{api_base_url}/accounts"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    status_codes = []
    
    for i in range(80):
        resp = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
        status_codes.append(resp.status_code)
        if resp.status_code == 429:
            assert "retry-after" in resp.headers
    
    success_count = sum(1 for s in status_codes if s in [200, 201])
    throttled_count = sum(1 for s in status_codes if s == 429)
    
    assert success_count == 60
    assert throttled_count == 20
    assert not any(s >= 500 for s in status_codes)

tests.name: test_rate_limit_reset_after_window_expires
preconditions: User has exhausted rate limit in current window
actions: Send 60 requests to exhaust limit
Wait 61 seconds for window to reset
Send 1 additional request after window expiry
Verify response status
expected: Request at 61+ seconds returns 2xx status code
Counter transitions from throttled to allowed state
trace: R-FIN-024: verify rate limit window reset allows new requests
State transition: throttled -> allowed after 60-second window
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest
import time

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('reset_rate_limit')
def test_rate_limit_reset_after_window_expires(api_base_url, auth_token):
    endpoint = f"{api_base_url}/accounts"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    
    for i in range(60):
        resp = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
        assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
        time.sleep(0.9)
    
    time.sleep(2)
    
    resp_after = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
    assert resp_after.status_code in [200, 201]
    assert "retry-after" not in resp_after.headers

tests.name: test_rate_limit_per_user_isolation
preconditions: Two distinct users with separate auth tokens exist
Both users have fresh rate limit counters
actions: User A sends 50 requests
User B sends 50 requests concurrently
Both users send additional requests
Verify each user's limit is independent
expected: User A: request 51 succeeds (within their limit)
User B: request 51 succeeds (within their limit)
Rate limits do not cross-user boundaries
trace: R-FIN-024: verify rate limiting is per-user, not global
Equivalence partition: requests partitioned by user identity
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest
import concurrent.futures

def send_requests(endpoint, token, count):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    results = []
    for _ in range(count):
        resp = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
        results.append(resp.status_code)
    return results

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('reset_rate_limit')
def test_rate_limit_per_user_isolation(api_base_url, auth_token_a, auth_token_b):
    endpoint = f"{api_base_url}/accounts"
    
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as ex:
        fut_a = ex.submit(send_requests, endpoint, auth_token_a, 51)
        fut_b = ex.submit(send_requests, endpoint, auth_token_b, 51)
        results_a = fut_a.result(timeout=60)
        results_b = fut_b.result(timeout=60)
    
    assert all(s in [200, 201] for s in results_a)
    assert all(s in [200, 201] for s in results_b)
